item.title: backlog history output omits duration column when duration is 0
item.intent: backlog history must display duration consistently — missing duration for items completed outside normal sprint flow is confusing and breaks column alignment
item.description: cmd_backlog_history() formats the output table with ID, date, status, and duration columns. The duration field in sprints.jsonl is stored as an integer (seconds). When an item is archived without a valid startedAt timestamp (e.g. manually marked done, or archive written before sprint started), duration is 0. The display logic should show '0s' or '-' for zero-duration entries rather than silently omitting the column or showing raw '0', which breaks column alignment for scripting.
steps: Locate the jq format expression that renders the duration column
Change the duration display: if duration is 0 or null, show '-'; otherwise format as minutes and seconds (e.g. '2m 34s' for 154)
Verify: 'backlog history' shows '-' for items with duration 0 and formatted time for items with duration > 0
acceptanceCriteria: backlog history shows '-' for items where duration is 0 or null
backlog history formats non-zero duration as human-readable time (e.g. '2m 34s')
Column alignment is consistent across all rows
